ASBESTOS CALENDAR ON JAN-27-2011 IN DEPT.220, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D)AND(E). THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLTFS' MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A TRIAL DATE IS GRANTED PER CCP 36(D) ON THE CONDITIONS THAT DEFT RIO TINTO BORAX BE DISMISSED. PARTIES HAVE STIPULATED TO THE FILING OF SUMMARY MOTIONS WITH 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL 2 DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. MR. LYNCH'S DECLARATION AND THE MEDICAL RECORDS SUBMITTED WITH THE REPLY PAPERS REMEDY ANY STALENESS CONCERNS WITH THE DECL OF DR. PENG. THE PAPERS SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFFS ESTABLISH THEIR ENTITLEMENT TO A TRIAL ON A PREFERENCE BASIS. TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 4/25/11 AT 11:15AM IN DEPT. 220 WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE RIGHT OF ANY DEFT TO MOVE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE UPON A SHOWING THAT THE DEFT HAS HAD AN INSUFFICIENT TIME TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL AFTER MAKING EVERY REASONABLE EFFORT TO BE READY FOR 4/25/11 TRIAL DATE. DISCOVERY IS CLOSED ON THE TRIAL DATE. LAST DAY TO HEAR SUMMARY MOTIONS IS 4/14/11. PLTFS TO MAKE DEMANDS ON ALL DEFTS BY 4/8/11. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN RM 603 AT 9AM ON 4/15/11. CMC IS SET FOR 2/17/11 AT 3:30PM. PLTFS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFT BY 2/21/11. ALL DEFTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROP 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFT BY 3/14/11. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. PLTF TO DISCLOSE INFO RE ALL BANKRUPTCY TRUST CLAIMS THAT HAVE BEEN FILED W/IN 15 DAYS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: R. VELUZ; N

RULING:
DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF NON-PARTY RAYMOND DAVID DASCENZO, SR.

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to compel the deposition of non-party Raymond David Dascenzo, Sr. is denied. Assuming without deciding that proper notice was given for the subpoena on Mr. Dascenzo and the subpoena did not call for personal records for which a notice or privacy rights is required, the motion is denied due to lack of good cause to take the deposition. The conclusory statement in paragraphs 3 and 4 of Ms. Fiorentino's declaration are insufficient to overcome the objections asserted to the deposition notice. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and the Court ruled as follows: Defendant Ford Motor Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant's alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 4 on the grounds that there is triable issues of fact with regard to Mr. Lynch's testimony regarding Fomoco Brakes. Issue 3 is granted on the grounds that Taylor precludes the 8th cause of action as under the current California Law. Issue 5 (punitive damage) is denied on the grounds that defendant did not provide sufficient evidence to negate punitive damages on the point of the residence of Ford during the relevant period of time. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
